title: woodsia alpina subsp. bellii
intro: Woodsia alpina subsp. bellii is a type of fern, specifically a species of the genus Woodsia, commonly found in high-altitude regions. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to its high content of mucilage, a gel-like substance that soothes the digestive tract. The medicinal properties of Woodsia alpina subsp. bellii also provide health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory issues. Traditionally, this plant is prepared through herbal infusions and decoctions, often combined with other herbs, to create remedies for various health conditions.
